trade.summary: Bilateral trade is growing, driven by the EU-Korea Free Trade Agreement. France exports luxury goods, aircraft, and agri-food to South Korea. Korea exports electronics, automobiles, ships, and semiconductors to France. Korean cultural exports have surged through Korean Wave popularity in France.

diplomatic.summary: France and South Korea elevated their bilateral relationship in 2023. Shared concerns over North Korea and Russia's aggression bring the two closer. South Korea participates in G7 summits as a guest. France supports Korean peninsula diplomacy. Cultural ties through the Korean Wave have strengthened people-to-people bonds.

military.summary: Growing defence cooperation as South Korea emerges as a major arms exporter. France and South Korea discussed potential cooperation in submarine and naval technologies. South Korea exports artillery and ammunition to European partners. Limited direct French-Korean military exercises but cooperative in NATO-adjacent events.

financial.summary: Korean conglomerates (Samsung, LG, Hyundai) have significant investments in France including EV battery factories. French companies invest in Korea in luxury retail and energy. Korean FDI into France has surged with EV supply chain investments linked to EU industrial policy.

energy.summary: Cooperation in nuclear energy technology (France's EDF and Korea's KHNP compete globally but cooperate on standards). France exports some LNG indirectly. Cooperation in hydrogen technology and offshore wind growing.

scientific.summary: Active scientific exchange in AI, materials science, and nuclear fusion. Korean students represent one of the largest Asian student populations in France. Joint CNRS-Korean research institute agreements in place. Korean Wave has increased French interest in Korean culture and language.
